name: Ben Riley
abstract: Benjamin Alexander Riley Jr. (July 17, 1933 – November 18, 2017) was an American jazz drummer known primarily for his work with Sonny Rollins and Thelonious Monk.

concerts.label: Jazz: The Personal Dimension: Roland Hanna Trio
Newport Jazz Festival: Music of the New Breed
JVC Jazz Festival: Tribute to Sarah Vaughan
JVC Jazz Festival: A Lullaby for Birdland

about_quotes.speaker: Kenny Barron
about_quotes.evidence: It started out – as you mentioned, we had the trio, Ben Riley, Buster Williams, and myself. We had been part of the Ron Carter quartet, that rhythm section.
about_quotes.doc_title: Kenny Barron — Smithsonian Jazz Oral History

about_quotes.speaker: Ron Carter
about_quotes.evidence: I've always loved Ben Riley's playing... So, I said, 'Well, let me get a band together,' and I had known Kenny from playing two dates with him... So when I put the band together, I said maybe I'd hire Buster and try to get Ben.
about_quotes.doc_title: Ron Carter — Smithsonian Jazz Oral History

about_quotes.speaker: Dick Katz
about_quotes.evidence: it was Helen and I and my trio with Ben Riley, who’s, he’s my favorite right now, and a wonderful bass player named Tommy Williams
about_quotes.doc_title: Dick Katz interviewed by Monk Rowe, New York City, New York, January 7, 2002

about_quotes.speaker: Jamey Aebersold
about_quotes.evidence: that was the first one that I flew to New York to do with Kenny Barron and whoever it was, Ron Carter and Ben Riley.
about_quotes.doc_title: Jamey Aebersold interviewed by Monk Rowe, via Zoom, September 26, 2022

about_quotes.speaker: Mose Allison
about_quotes.evidence: The interviewer mentions Ben Riley in the context of Thelonious Monk, but the interviewee (Mose Allison) does not respond with any personal connection or interaction with Ben Riley.
about_quotes.doc_title: Mose Allison — Smithsonian Jazz Oral History

about_quotes.speaker: Stanley Kay
about_quotes.evidence: I took her out of the Mel Lewis-Thad Jones band, two Franks, Frank Foster and Frank Wess, Roland Hanna, Ben Riley and a guy that played like that
about_quotes.doc_title: Stanley Kay interviewed by Monk Rowe, Clearwater Beach, Florida, March 18, 2001
